element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational combat mechanic used throughout entire game
Defense against ranged attacks; ineffective vs melee
Progression system; stat scaling continues throughout game
Economy for weapons, armor, healing items
One-time use spell casting; must recollect Magirocks after each cast
Escalating dungeon difficulty; boss encounters with new magic unlocks
World transition mechanic; introduces Lightside (surface world)

element_introduction_order.element: Giant tree Ra parasite puzzle (environmental obstacle)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: First major environmental puzzle; unlocks plant resurrection

element_introduction_order.element: Sequential life resurrection (plants → birds → wind → animals → humans)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Paced world opening mechanic; each stage enables new areas
Town building progression; enables technology invention tasks
Side quest system for world development; unlocks new traversal
Narrative deepening; emotional stakes for final conflict
Final antagonist introduction; reveals Asmodeus virus threat

typical_tension_triggers: Tower dungeon entry (escalating difficulty per tower)
Beruga awakening and Asmodeus virus revelation
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Three-act structure with building world stakes: Chapter 1 establishes confined Darkside restoration, Chapter 2 expands to Lightside wilderness recovery with environmental puzzles, Chapter 3 escalates through civilization development toward existential threat. Tension releases at chapter transitions and major world openings.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Secondary NPC quests and city expansion tasks; non-linear town progression paths during Chapter 2-3
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Forbidden door opening in Crysta triggers narrative (likely inverts typical 'avoid closed doors' convention); unused items suggest hidden puzzle solutions were planned (Heaven Statue for rainfall, Powered Gear for heavy lifting)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-paced Chapter 1 introduces mechanics in Crysta safe zone before five towers; tower difficulty escalates progressively
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Chapter 2 Lightside traversal increases enemy complexity; environmental puzzles (Ra parasite) require strategic thinking; mountain crossing demands sustained combat
difficulty_curve.late_game: Chapter 3 civilization assistance quests become more complex; Beruga encounter dramatically raises stakes; final confrontation with existential threat (Asmodeus virus)
notable_spikes: Five towers sequence (Chapter 1 finale) - each tower represents escalating combat/environmental challenge
Guiana mountain crossing - resource management pressure after Lightside transition
Beruga awakening (Chapter 3) - reveals true antagonist and endgame threat scope

notable_level_patterns.description: Five parallel dungeon structures (one per continent), each requiring boss defeat to unlock continent resurrection. Acts as paced gating mechanism; returning player must complete towers in sequence to progress, each adding world-opening rewards.

Chapter 2 gates traversal behind sequential life form resurrections: plant life unlocks mountains, animal resurrection enables new terrain passage, human civilization opening access to cities. Each unlock meaningfully expands traversable space.
Major obstacles (Ra parasite infestation) block progression until solved; solution unlocks entire life form category and new map regions. Ties puzzle solution directly to world state changes.

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Hub Return and Safe Zone Pacing
notable_level_patterns.description: Crysta village serves as return point in Chapter 1; Chapter 2-3 introduce multiple hub cities that gradually populate. Provides emotional/mechanical breather between dangerous zones.

sources_used: tcrf - Terranigma (Unseen Areas, Unused Items - reveals cut puzzle mechanics)
